claimText: India is the third Southeast Asian country to receive the WHO Certificate of Elimination of Trachoma as a Public Health Problem.
evidenceExcerpt: India was awarded the WHO Certificate of Elimination of Trachoma as a Public Health Problem, becoming the 3rd SE Asian country to do so.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's public health achievements and WHO certifications.
distractorHooks: India is the first Southeast Asian country to eliminate Trachoma
India is the only country in the world to eliminate Trachoma

claimText: Trachoma is an eye disease caused by the bacterium Chlamydia trachomatis.
evidenceExcerpt: Trachoma is an eye disease caused by Chlamydia trachomatis... Trachoma is caused by the bacterium Chlamydia trachomatis
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question identifying the causative agents of specific diseases.
distractorHooks: Trachoma is caused by a virus
Trachoma is caused by a fungal infection

claimText: Trachoma is classified as a Neglected Tropical Disease (NTD) by the World Health Organization.
evidenceExcerpt: categorized as a Neglected Tropical Disease (NTD)... It is classified as a Neglected Tropical Disease (NTD) by the WHO.
examUse: Statement-based question on the classification of diseases as NTDs.
distractorHooks: Trachoma is classified as a non-communicable disease
Trachoma was recently removed from the WHO's list of Neglected Tropical Diseases

claimText: Trachoma is an infection that leads to irreversible blindness.
examUse: Statement-based question on the clinical outcomes and severity of Trachoma.
distractorHooks: Trachoma primarily affects the nervous system

claimText: Trachoma elimination efforts in India are integrated under the National Programme for Control of Blindness and Visual Impairment (NPCBVI).
examUse: Institution-feature question linking specific diseases to their respective national health programs.
distractorHooks: Trachoma elimination is integrated under the National Vector Borne Disease Control Programme
Trachoma elimination is managed as a standalone vertical program outside of national blindness initiatives
